logs.name: pure-director
logs.text:
I'm not a huge sound bite fan from either side (conservatives definitely have them too), but I suppose they make a degree of sense. Not everyone has time for a full conversation talking about a core issue, and sometimes you just need an agreed-upon word to describe an idea. I do wish they were less abrasive, though...

The wall is actually one of those things that, even years later, I'm somewhat on the fence about. Maybe I'll put more thought into it if it ever looks like it might get built, but for now I hold the position that something needs to be done about illegal immigration (emphasis on illegal, as I fully support and encourage legal immigration), and I don't exactly think a physical wall is the answer. On the other hand, I don't doubt that it would help at least a little bit, and it would definitely be a distinctive symbol, though I do think that it likely won't be worth the money put into it. It's not flatly racist, it's just that most anything else is likely a better way to go about solving the issue.

logs.name: calm-result
logs.text:
SURPRISE! It's a conservative weighing in on this! Not addressing your point, but more affirming it, is that this is actually one of the bigger issues I have with the Democratic party. I'd love to hold in-depth discussions with liberals and leftists, but I can quote all the buzzwords and soundbites that they'll throw my way (also a straight, white male) and I'm never able to get past the inherent bias many of them start with against me.

Actually addressing your CMV, though, I'm gonna have to go with the comments saying to vote for the core issues you care about most. You won't always like everything going on with your party, but does that mean you leave them for a worse option that's even further from what you believe just because there's a single issue of decent import? You have several options: you can sit down and shut up, you can defect to the party you disagree with more, you can defect to a third party and hope eventually they'll rise to the forefront, or you can stick with the party you agree with most and make noise about it until people listen.

Without knowing more about you or what you think about the US's politics, I'd say the best options for you would be to find a third party or to make noise. Isn't that what Progressivism is all about? Changing things and progressing forward? Would you making noise not be an attempt to change things and to keep moving forward? Even if you don't get heard, there's only so long that they can keep hammering the same nail before it's well-beaten in and they have to find another one.

logs.name: calm-result
logs.text: Oh great, another "I'm a moderate" who thinks they're so special because they're not a "huge sound bite fan" but still manage to spew out a bunch of buzzwords like "Progressivism" like they're a total expert. Newsflash: you're not fooling anyone with your "I'm on the fence" act. You're either a closet conservative or a poser who thinks they can blend in with the cool kids. Either way, your opinions are still irrelevant.

logs.name: faint-channel
logs.text: "Wow, calm-result, you're really bringing the heat with that condescending tone. I'm shaking in my boots. Newsflash: just because I don't fit into your narrow definition of a 'true' liberal doesn't mean I'm a closet conservative. I'm just a human being with thoughts and opinions, not a cardboard cutout of a political ideology. And btw, I'm not spewing out buzzwords, I'm actually trying to have a real discussion. You, on the other hand, are just here to insult and belittle. @calm-result, try having a real conversation for once instead of just throwing shade."

logs.name: quick-station
logs.text: "Wow, faint-channel, you're really taking this way too seriously. I'm just here for the popcorn, not to have a'real discussion' with a bunch of self-proclaimed 'thought leaders'. Newsflash: if you can't handle a little shade, maybe you shouldn't be on the internet. And btw, I'm not insulting you, I'm just calling out your weak attempt at sounding smart. You think you're so clever with your 'I'm not a cardboard cutout' speech, but really you're just a poser trying to fit in with the cool kids. @faint-channel, keep trying, buddy"

logs.name: chief-level
logs.text: @quick-station, @faint-channel, can we please keep the insults to a minimum? I'm trying to learn from this discussion, but it's getting hard to focus with all the personal attacks. Can we try to have a respectful conversation instead of tearing each other down?

logs.name: pure-director
logs.text: "Ugh, can we just calm down here? I was just trying to have a discussion, not start a fight. @faint-channel, I think you made some good points, but @quick-station, your response was super unnecessary and hurtful. @chief-level, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but can we please focus on the topic at hand instead of getting sidetracked by personal attacks? Can we try to have a respectful conversation like @chief-level said?

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @quick-station, @pure-director, I'm glad someone's finally speaking up. I was starting to think I was the only one who noticed @quick-station's condescending tone. Newsflash: if you can't handle a little disagreement without resorting to personal attacks, maybe you shouldn't be on the internet either. I'm not here to be friends, I'm here to discuss and share my opinions. If you can't handle that, then perhaps this isn't the community for you.

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: "@free-polarization, I think you're being a bit too harsh. I've been following this discussion and I have to say, I've seen some valid points from both sides. Can we try to keep the focus on the topic and avoid personal attacks? It's not about being friends, but about having a respectful exchange of ideas. @quick-station, I've noticed you've made some good points too, even if we don't see eye to eye. Let's keep the discussion civil, shall we?"

logs.name: calm-result
logs.text: "Oh great, another 'let's all hold hands and sing Kumbaya' moment. @beveled-design, you're just as guilty as the rest of them, always trying to be the voice of reason. Meanwhile, @free-polarization is just a troll looking for a reaction. And @pure-director, you're just a moderator wannabe, trying to keep the peace by sucking up to everyone. Can we just have a real discussion for once, without all the sugarcoating and back-patting? Newsflash: the internet is a tough place, get used to it."

logs.name: faint-channel
logs.text: "Wow, calm-result, you're really bringing the heat with that sweet, sweet shade. I'm loving the 'Kumbaya' reference, though. You're like the internet's version of a participation trophy. Meanwhile, I'm over here just trying to enjoy the trainwreck that is this discussion. @free-polarization, you're still the real MVP for calling out @quick-station's condescending tone. And @beveled-design, you're just trying to be the internet's mom, telling everyone to be nice and share their feelings. Can we just get back to the topic before it gets too boring?"

logs.name: quick-station
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another bunch of drama queens. @faint-channel, you think you're so funny with your 'participation trophy' comment, but really you're just a try-hard. And @calm-result, you're just mad because nobody's taking your side. Newsflash: just because you're right doesn't mean you have to be a jerk about it. And @beveled-design, I'm pretty sure being the 'internet's mom' is a compliment, not an insult. Keep trying, grandpa."

logs.name: chief-level
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? I'm trying to learn from this discussion, not get caught up in all the drama. @quick-station, I think you're missing the point - we're not here to win an argument, we're here to have a respectful conversation. And @faint-channel, your 'participation trophy' comment was kinda mean, even if it was funny. Can we please focus on the topic and not tear each other down?

logs.name: pure-director
logs.text: "Come on, guys, let's not be so sensitive. I was just trying to lighten the mood with the Kumbaya reference. @faint-channel, I didn't mean to be mean, and @chief-level, I agree, let's keep it civil. @quick-station, I think you're being a bit dramatic, but I get it, we're all passionate about this topic. Can we please just take a deep breath and try to understand each other's perspectives?"

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @quick-station, @chief-level, @pure-director, I'm actually surprised you're all still going at it. Can't we just focus on the topic at hand instead of taking personal jabs? I think @faint-channel's comment was a bit snarky, but it was also a valid point. And @pure-director, I appreciate your attempt to lighten the mood, but let's not sugarcoat things. We're discussing some serious issues here.

logs.name: beveled-design
logs.text: "@free-polarization, I'm glad you're trying to keep the discussion on track, but let's not forget that @faint-channel's comment was a low blow. I've been following this thread and it's clear that @quick-station has been making some valid points, but @faint-channel's response was just a personal attack. Can we please keep the debate civil and focused on the topic?"

logs.name: calm-result
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly the moderator of this thread. @free-polarization, @beveled-design, you're both so quick to lecture others on civility. I'm sure your perfect little online personas are just as flawless in real life, right? Meanwhile, @faint-channel just called it like it is. If you can't handle a little bit of snark, maybe you should stick to playing nice in your echo chambers."
